name: Rice and Oyster Dressing
recipeIngredient: 10 cups Cooked rice in chicken broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a large skillet.
2. Add garlic, onions, bell pepper, and celery to the butter and sauté lightly.
3. Remove the vegetables from the pan, retaining the butter.
4. Add oysters and sauté in the remaining butter until edges curl. If oysters are small, they may remain whole; large ones can be chopped after sautéing.
5. Remove oysters and set aside.
6. Wash chicken livers thoroughly and place them in a pan with enough water to just cover them. Boil until done, then drain and slice finely.
7. Combine all ingredients (except beaten eggs) in a large bowl and mix well with your hands. Adjust seasonings as needed by adding more salt or cayenne pepper.
8. Add the six beaten eggs and mix thoroughly. The mixture should hold together without being too wet or dry. Adjust moisture by draining liquid or adding additional chicken broth.
9. Pour the mixture into a 4-quart casserole and cover. Warm in an oven preheated to 325°F for about 20 minutes. This recipe can also be used to stuff a turkey weighing 25 pounds and cooked accordingly. Serves 15 people.
